description_long: This is a cylindrical fire hydrant painted primarily white, with a domed top and a flanged base secured by bolts. It features three hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one on the front, each with a cap, and the front cap is attached to a short chain. There are visible signs of rust around the outlet joints and bolts, indicating exposure to the elements. A large blue teardrop-shaped marking is painted on the front, and the number '8' is stenciled in black on the body. The hydrant also displays some manufacturer or model markings in raised or stamped text near the base. The surface is hard, slightly rough, and shows some wear and weathering typical of outdoor urban infrastructure.
description: A weathered white fire hydrant with three capped outlets, blue and black markings, and visible rust around the joints and bolts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet with a chained cap, black '8' marking, and some manufacturer text near the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the blue teardrop marking and two side outlets, one with a chained cap.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the hydrant with less visible markings and the side outlets.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible manufacturer/model text and the side outlets, one with a chained cap.
